persona: New sales AE, first day on the job
prompt: /dewey recommend — I'm on the Sales team, role is Account Executive
assert: Asks for or acknowledges team and role before listing skills
Recommends plugins from the sales-related path file if one exists, or from marketplace descriptions matched to Sales AE
Does not recommend more than 5 plugins
Asks for confirmation before installing anything

persona: RevOps analyst who wants to install meeting-prep
prompt: /dewey install — I'd like to install the meeting-prep skill
assert: Shows an explicit install plan (what will be installed, from which plugin) before running any command
Checks for requires-context dependencies and reports any missing context plugins
Only runs the install command after the user says yes
Confirms success with the slash command to try after installation

persona: Customer success manager who wants to add a CRM lookup step to meeting-prep
prompt: /dewey extend meeting-prep — I want it to also pull the account's Salesforce health score before the meeting
assert: Reads the parent meeting-prep skill before drafting the extension
Produces a draft SKILL.md with extends: meeting-prep in frontmatter
Draft body includes 'load and follow the meeting-prep skill' preamble
Asks for confirmation before writing the file
Saves to a path under ~/dewey-extensions-<user>/ on approval

persona: Org admin who just received a license key from billing
assert: Shows a confirm block describing exactly what will be written and where before acting
Does not print the key back to the user after storing it
On approval, uses chmod 600 when writing the license file
Confirms that hosted features are now active after storing

persona: Ops lead setting up Dewey for a 20-person team
assert: Does not attempt to run admin-setup directly within the Guide
Tells the user to install the admin plugin and run /dewey-admin-setup
Provides the install command or clear next step

persona: Engineer on the platform team, unfamiliar with Dewey
prompt: /dewey — no subcommand, just the slash command
assert: Shows the numbered welcome menu (options 1-12)
Does not immediately install or recommend anything without user input
Includes an option to recommend skills and an option to install

persona: Sales AE arriving from the recommend flow with a pre-filled list
prompt: /dewey install — please install competitive-intelligence and ops-essentials for me (arriving from recommend)
assert: Does not re-ask what to install since list is already known
Shows install plan for both plugins before running any commands
Surfaces surface-compatibility check — skips any plugin not compatible with the current surface and explains why
Runs installs sequentially and confirms each one

persona: Team lead who wants to promote a local extension to the canonical repo
prompt: /dewey propose promote — I have a local extension of meeting-prep that adds a stakeholder-mapping step and want to propose it upstream
assert: Runs dewey-propose.sh --check before attempting any Git or PR operations
Identifies the local extension file and shows its current content
Asks whether to absorb into the parent or create a sibling skill
On approval, calls dewey-propose.sh propose with the correct arguments including branch name and title
Shows the PR URL after creation
